en: Therefore you have no excuse, O man, every one of you who judges. For in passing judgment on another you condemn yourself, because you, the judge, practice the very same things.
zh: 你這判斷人的啊！無論你是誰，都沒有辦法推諉。你在甚麼事上判斷人，就在甚麼事上定自己的罪；因為你所作的，正是你所判斷的事。

en: We know that the judgment of God rightly falls on those who do such things.
zh: 我們知道， 神必照著真理審判行這些事的人。

en: Do you suppose, O man- you who judge those who do such things and yet do them yourself- that you will escape the judgment of God?
zh: 你這個人，你判斷行這些事的人，自己所行的卻是一樣，你以為能逃脫 神的審判嗎？

en: Or do you presume on the riches of his kindness and forbearance and patience, not knowing that God's kindness is meant to lead you to repentance?
zh: 還是你藐視 神豐富的恩慈、寬容和忍耐，不曉得他的恩慈是要領你悔改的嗎？

en: But because of your hard and impenitent heart you are storing up wrath for yourself on the day of wrath when God's righteous judgment will be revealed.
zh: 可是你一直硬著心腸，不肯悔改，為自己積蓄 神的忿怒，就是他彰顯公義審判的那天所要發的忿怒。

en: He will render to each one according to his works:
zh: 神必照各人所作的報應各人：

en: to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality, he will give eternal life;
zh: 以永生報答那些耐心行善、尋求榮耀尊貴和不朽的人，

en: but for those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, there will be wrath and fury.
zh: 卻以震怒和憤恨報應那些自私自利、不順從真理而順從不義的人；

en: There will be tribulation and distress for every human being who does evil, the Jew first and also the Greek,
zh: 把患難和愁苦加給所有作惡的人，先是猶太人，後是希臘人，

en: but glory and honor and peace for everyone who does good, the Jew first and also the Greek.
zh: 卻把榮耀、尊貴與平安賜給所有行善的人，先是猶太人，後是希臘人。

en: For God shows no partiality.
zh: 因為 神並不偏待人。

en: For all who have sinned without the law will also perish without the law, and all who have sinned under the law will be judged by the law.
zh: 凡不在律法之下犯了罪的，將不按律法而滅亡；凡在律法之下犯了罪的，將按律法受審判。

en: For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God, but the doers of the law who will be justified.
zh: 因為在 神面前，不是聽律法的為義，而是行律法的得稱為義。

en: For when Gentiles, who do not have the law, by nature do what the law requires, they are a law to themselves, even though they do not have the law.
zh: 沒有律法的外族人，如果按本性行律法上的事，他們雖然沒有律法，自己就是自己的律法；

en: They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts, while their conscience also bears witness, and their conflicting thoughts accuse or even excuse them
zh: 這就表明律法的作用是刻在他們的心裡，有他們的良心一同作證，他們的思想互相較量，或作控告、或作辯護。

en: on that day when, according to my gospel, God judges the secrets of men by Christ Jesus.
zh: 這也要照著我所傳的福音，在 神藉著耶穌基督審判各人隱情的那一天，彰顯出來。

en: But if you call yourself a Jew and rely on the law and boast in God
zh: 你身為猶太人，倚靠律法，仗著 神誇口，

en: and know his will and approve what is excellent, because you are instructed from the law;
zh: 而且明白他的旨意，又從律法得了教導，能夠辨別甚麼是好的，

en: and if you are sure that you yourself are a guide to the blind, a light to those who are in darkness,
zh: 自信是瞎子的嚮導，在黑暗中的人的光，

en: an instructor of the foolish, a teacher of children, having in the law the embodiment of knowledge and truth-
zh: 愚昧人的導師，小孩子的教師，在律法上得了整套的知識和真理；

en: you then who teach others, do you not teach yourself? While you preach against stealing, do you steal?
zh: 你既然教導別人，難道不教導自己嗎？你傳講不可偷竊，自己卻偷竊嗎？

en: You who say that one must not commit adultery, do you commit adultery? You who abhor idols, do you rob temples?
zh: 你說不可姦淫，自己卻姦淫嗎？你憎惡偶像，自己卻劫掠廟宇嗎？

en: You who boast in the law dishonor God by breaking the law.
zh: 你既然以律法誇口，自己卻因犯律法而羞辱 神嗎？

en: For, as it is written, "The name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you."
zh: 正如經上所說的：“ 神的名，因你們的緣故在列邦中被褻瀆。”

en: For circumcision indeed is of value if you obey the law, but if you break the law, your circumcision becomes uncircumcision.
zh: 你若遵行律法，割禮固然有益處；但你若是犯律法的，你的割禮就不是割禮了。

en: So, if a man who is uncircumcised keeps the precepts of the law, will not his uncircumcision be regarded as circumcision?
zh: 這樣，沒有受割禮的人，如果遵守律法所規定的，他雖然沒有受過割禮，不也算是受過割禮的嗎？

en: Then he who is physically uncircumcised but keeps the law will condemn you who have the written code and circumcision but break the law.
zh: 那本來沒有受割禮卻遵守律法的人，就要審判你這有儀文和割禮而犯律法的人。

en: For no one is a Jew who is merely one outwardly, nor is circumcision outward and physical.
zh: 因為表面上作猶太人的並不是猶太人，在肉身上表面的割禮也不是割禮。

en: But a Jew is one inwardly, and circumcision is a matter of the heart, by the Spirit, not by the letter. His praise is not from man but from God.
zh: 唯有在內心作猶太人的才是猶太人；割禮也是心裡的，是靠著聖靈而不是靠著儀文。這樣的人所受的稱讚，不是從人來的，而是從 神來的。
